medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Former smoker with HTN, thyroid nodule, and anxiety presents with headache. She came to the emergency room because she had a headache and thought her BP must be elevated. She does not have a history of headaches and the last time she had a headache, her BP was elevated so she felt it must be the case this time. Denies vision changes, lightheadedness or chest pain. No numbness/tingling except for some stiff/tingling left fingers this afternoon after waking from a nap, but it quickly resolved. In the ED, initial vitals were remarkable for SaO2 of 90% RA. Pt denies SOB. HA resolved with sublingual nitroglycerin given by EMS. HTN treated in ED with a total of 15mg lopressor IV. Ambulatory SaO2 was 90% RA. CXR showed new large R pleural effusion, with possible air bronchograms. Pt given levofloxacin and admitted for workup of new pleural effusion.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Thyroid nodule, anxiety, type 2 diabetes, hyperlipidemia, hypertension, right subclavian stenosis, chronic kidney disease, bilateral less than 40% ICA stenosis.

physical_examination.general_examination: Afebrile BP: 190/92 (R arm), 240/100 (L arm) P: 72 O2: 94% 2L (90% RA). General: Pleasant, alert, oriented, appears anxious. CV: RRR, normal S1 + S2, systolic murmur at RUSB and LUSB, no rubs or gallops.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: CN II-XII intact, able to follow simple commands, no dysarthria. CV: Bronchial breath sounds on R, basilar rales on L, dullness to percussion, trace nonpitting edema of L leg.
